story: A food lover from Jodhpur who studied in Bhopal and now lives in Bengaluru, chasing meals with warmth, spice, and comfort while making PG cooking feel intentional instead of repetitive.
cookingReason: Recently learned cooking after getting bored of repetitive PG rice meals and wants practical but exciting daily food.
defaultPantry: rice, atta, onions, tomatoes, curd, ginger, garlic, green chilli, jeera, turmeric, chilli powder, garam masala, besan, eggs

Breakfast.name: Bengaluru-style ragi chilli cheela
Breakfast.why: Fast, filling, and ideal for a beginner who wants something better than plain toast or leftover rice.
tasks: Mix a quick batter with ragi or besan, curd, chilli, and chopped onion.
Make a fast chutney or curd dip while the pan heats.
Cook two thin cheelas and pack extra dip for later use.
Breakfast.memoryHook: A Bengaluru breakfast rhythm adapted for a compact PG kitchen.

Lunch.name: Bhopal comfort dal bowl
Lunch.why: Comforting enough for a long day, but simplified for one person and a tight student-style setup.
tasks: Pressure cook dal with tomato, garlic, and turmeric.
Prepare a quick tadka and fold it into the dal.
Serve with soft atta bites, rice, or toasted leftovers based on budget.
Lunch.memoryHook: Captures the comforting college-food feeling of Bhopal without a complicated process.

Dinner.name: Jodhpur masala khichdi skillet
Dinner.why: Turns a rice-heavy dinner into something spicy, richer, and more memorable with very little extra effort.
tasks: Cook rice and moong dal together until soft.
Build a masala base with onion, tomato, chilli, and spices.
Fold the base into the khichdi and finish with curd or protein.
Dinner.memoryHook: Jodhpur warmth and spice, made manageable for a weekday night.
